    ],

      "それはゴロデツ・オン・ザ・ヴォルガの近くの修道院にありました。",
      "それは生神女就寝大聖堂にありました。",
      "それはコストロマの低地の復活教会にありました。",
      "それはコストロマのエピファニー大聖堂にありました。"
    ],
    "HeldOn": [
      "それは1239年8月16日にありました。"

      "それは奇跡のイコンです。",
      "それはロマノフ王朝の聖なる守護者です。",
      "それは黒い聖母です。",
      "それは守護聖像です。"

      "それはロマノフ王朝300周年との関連で知られています。"

About: It is about the Mother of God.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Romanov family.
CreatedAround: It was created around the turn of the 14th century.
It was created around the early 11th-century.
CreatedIn: It was created in the Novgorod Republic.
DiscoveredAt: It was discovered at a forest.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by Prince Vasily of Kostroma.
HasA: It has an image representing Saint Paraskeva on the reverse side.
It has a Byzantine Eleusa type.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its veneration in the Upper Volga region.
It is characterized by being the patron icon of the Romanov family.
It is characterized by its blackening image.
It is characterized by its veneration in Gorodets and Kostroma.
It is characterized by its veneration in nearby Yaroslavl.
It is characterized by its veneration during the annual Makariev Fair in Nizhny Novgorod.
It is characterized by Western Christian women who married into the House of Romanov taking Feodorovna as patronymic in honor of it.
HeldIn: It was held in an old wooden chapel near the city of Kitezh.
It was held in a monastery near the town of Gorodets-on-the-Volga.
It was held in the Assumption Cathedral.
It was held in the Resurrection Church on the Lowlands in Kostroma.
It was held in the Epiphany Cathedral in Kostroma.
IsA: It is one of the most venerated icons in the Upper Volga region.
It is a copy of the Theotokos of Vladimir.
It is a holy protectress of the Romanov dynasty.
KnownAs: It is known as Our Lady of Saint Theodore.
It is known as the Black Virgin Mary of Russia.
KnownFor: It is known for being the patron icon of the Romanov family.
It is known for its veneration in the Upper Volga region.
It is known for its survival of a fire.
It is known for its connection to Saint Theodore Stratelates.
It is known for its veneration by the Romanov dynasty.
It is known for its veneration in Yaroslavl.
It is known for its veneration in Gorodets.
It is known for its veneration during the Makariev Fair.
It is known for Western Christian women marrying into the Romanov family taking Feodorovna as patronymic.
It is known for its association with the tercentenary of the Romanov dynasty.
NamedAfter: It is named after Saint Theodore Stratelates.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Mongol invasion of Rus.
It occurred during the Russian Revolution of 1917.
It occurred during the tercentenary of the Romanov dynasty in 1913.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the city of Kostroma.
UsedAs: It is used as a patron icon.
It is used as a holy protectress of the Romanov dynasty.
UsedFor: It is used for protection of Mikhail and his royal descendants.
UsedIn: It is used in the Upper Volga region.
It is used in the Assumption Cathedral.
It is used in the Resurrection Church on the Lowlands in Kostroma.
It is used in the Epiphany Cathedral in Kostroma.
WrittenBy: It was written by Saint Luke.
